title: Verna — golden mohair teddy restoration
description: Verna is a 1960s golden mohair jointed teddy — worn pile, loose joints, and tired stuffing. We cleaned, restuffed, and repaired seams so she sits upright again with her original charm.
itemDetails:
Verna is a named 1960s vintage teddy bear in the post-war plush tradition — golden mohair pile on cotton backing, five-point jointing (head, arms, legs) typical of mid-century gift and collector bears. The golden colour and mohair texture match the era when regional makers still used natural-style pile before full synthetics dominated.

Construction usually combines disk or cotter-pin joints, wood-wool or polyester fill (often replaced during restoration), and glass or early plastic eyes depending on maker. Verna-style named bears bridge older German/British teddy patterns and 1960s safer-child construction. Collector value depends on provenance, joint tightness, originality of pads and eyes, and condition of the mohair; documented 1960s named bears with intact character appeal are sought after in the nostalgia market.

Photos for this repair were previously filed under USB folders Vintage_yellow_bear / Vintage_bear_straw (now project 0043).
